title: US-Israel strikes Bandar Khamir port near Hormuz; 5 killed, 4 injured
description: US-Israeli forces struck the Bandar Pol dock in Bandar Khamir County, a port city near the Strait of Hormuz, in the early hours of Sunday. Iran's IRNA news agency reported 5 killed and 4 injured. Separately, loud explosions were heard across Tehran early Sunday, with smoke rising over northern neighborhoods including Saadat Abad. Iran's Mehr and Fars agencies reported 2 killed and 5 wounded in Shaft city village, 3 wounded in Saadat Abad, and 9 wounded in western Tehran from overnight strikes.

title: IDF completes 3 'widespread' strike waves on Tehran in single day
description: The IDF completed three successive waves of strikes across Tehran on Saturday, targeting what it described as ballistic missile production infrastructure, storage sites, and command centers "in the heart of Tehran." The IDF posted on X that it had completed "a widespread wave of strikes" on Iranian government infrastructure. US CENTCOM chief and the IDF Chief of Staff discussed the ongoing joint campaign, which has largely focused on destroying Iran's weapons production industries.

title: Iran missile hits ADAMA chemical plant in Ne'ot Hovav; hazmat teams deployed
description: An Iranian missile struck the ADAMA Makhteshim pesticide plant in Ne'ot Hovav industrial zone, ~13 km from Beersheba, causing a large fire and hazardous chemical leak. Three specialized hazmat teams were deployed; the deputy head of the fire service's Southern District identified the leaking material as a pesticide. The plant also contains ammonia storage tanks. Staff were evacuated and Highway 40 was closed. One person was lightly wounded. A separate Iranian ballistic missile hit an open area in Beersheba, lightly injuring 11 people and damaging buildings.

title: IRGC strikes EGA aluminium smelter in UAE and Alba plant in Bahrain
description: The IRGC claimed strikes on Emirates Global Aluminium's Al Taweelah smelter in Abu Dhabi and Aluminium Bahrain (Alba), in stated retaliation for US-Israeli strikes on Iranian steel plants. EGA reported "significant damage" at Al Taweelah — the Middle East's largest aluminium producer (~4% of global output). Six EGA workers were injured; 2 Alba employees sustained minor injuries. Alba had already shut 3 smelting lines (19% of capacity) due to Hormuz disruptions. The UAE intercepted 16 ballistic missiles and 42 drones on Sunday; total Iranian attacks on the UAE since the war began: 414 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, and 1,914 drones.

title: Houthis fire second missile at Israel within 24 hours; both intercepted
description: The Houthis carried out a second missile and drone attack on Israel on Sunday, less than 24 hours after their first strike on Saturday. Both attacks were intercepted; no casualties reported. Houthi Brig. Gen. Yahya Saree said the attack "coincided with the military operations being carried out" by Hezbollah and Iran. The Houthis have not yet closed the Bab al-Mandeb Strait but deputy information minister Mohammed Mansour said closure is "expected in the next phase if Israel decides to target the port of Hodeidah or Yemeni civilian and public infrastructure."

title: IRGC declares US and Israeli universities in Middle East 'legitimate targets'
description: The IRGC declared all American and Israeli universities in the Middle East "legitimate targets" in retaliation for US-Israeli strikes on Tehran's University of Science and Technology and Isfahan University of Technology. The IRGC demanded Washington officially condemn the university strikes by Monday at noon, or it would begin targeting US and Israeli campuses. It warned students and staff to stay at least 1 km from campuses. At-risk institutions include Texas A&M University Qatar and NYU Abu Dhabi. Georgetown University in Qatar had already shifted to remote instruction.

title: Netanyahu orders expansion of Lebanon buffer zone; 250 Hezbollah rockets over weekend
description: PM Netanyahu, speaking from the Northern Command, ordered the IDF to "further expand the existing security zone" in Lebanon "to finally thwart the threat of invasion and to push the anti-tank missile fire away from our border." He cited "three security zones deep within enemy territory" — Lebanon, Gaza, and Syria — as a paradigm shift. Hezbollah launched approximately 250 projectiles from Lebanon over the weekend. It was not immediately clear whether the expansion extends beyond the previously announced Litani River line. Lebanon's death toll since March 2: ~1,200 killed, 3,400+ wounded.

title: Pakistan hosts 4-nation FM talks (Turkey, Egypt, Saudi) to prepare US-Iran engagement
description: Foreign ministers of Pakistan (Ishaq Dar), Turkey (Hakan Fidan), Egypt (Badr Abdelatty), and Saudi Arabia (Prince Faisal bin Farhan) began two-day talks in Islamabad aimed at aligning regional positions and preparing the ground for possible direct US-Iran engagement. Pakistan offered to host US-Iran talks "in coming days." FM Dar announced Iran had agreed to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged ships through the Strait of Hormuz, with 2 ships crossing daily. China conveyed support for Pakistan's mediation. Iran's parliament speaker Qalibaf dismissed the talks as cover while the US deploys more troops.

title: WaPo: Pentagon readies 'weeks of ground operations' in Iran, including Kharg Island raids
description: The Washington Post reported the Pentagon is preparing for weeks of limited ground operations in Iran, including special operations and conventional infantry raids on Kharg Island and coastal sites near the Strait of Hormuz. The operations aim to find and destroy weapons targeting commercial and military shipping. One official estimated objectives would take "weeks, not months"; another said "a couple of months." White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt stated: "It's the job of the Pentagon to make preparations... It does not mean the president has made a decision." Some 1,500 82nd Airborne paratroopers are preparing to deploy.

title: Houthis enter war: first missile at Israel since Gaza ceasefire; IDF intercepts
description: Yemen's Houthi rebels launched ballistic missiles at "sensitive Israeli military sites" in southern Israel — their first attack since the October 2025 Gaza ceasefire and first involvement in the Iran war. The IDF intercepted the projectile; no casualties were reported. Houthi Brig. Gen. Yahya Saree announced on Al Masirah TV that strikes "will continue until the aggression against all fronts of the resistance ceases." Senior Houthi official Mohammed Mansour warned that closing the Bab al-Mandeb Strait "is among our options," which would shut a second global chokepoint alongside the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Iran strikes Prince Sultan Air Base: 12-15 US troops wounded, AWACS and KC-135 damaged
description: Iran fired 6 ballistic missiles and 29 drones at Saudi Arabia's Prince Sultan Air Base near Riyadh, wounding at least 12-15 US service members — at least 5 in serious condition. A USAF E-3 Sentry AWACS command-and-control plane and at least one KC-135 air refueling tanker were damaged; the KC-135 caught fire. The attack brought total US casualties since February 28 to 13 killed and over 300 wounded. The Pentagon and CENTCOM did not immediately comment.

title: Iranian cluster missile kills man in Tel Aviv; shrapnel hits Yehuda Halevi Street
description: An Iranian cluster missile struck central Tel Aviv at approximately 11:45 PM local time, scattering bomblets across Yehuda Halevi Street. Vyacheslav Vidmant, 52, of Ashdod — employed as a municipal guard at buildings evacuated after a prior missile strike — was killed by shrapnel. He had finished his shift at 10 PM but remained on site and was not in a shelter when sirens sounded. Two others were wounded, one moderately. The barrage also triggered sirens in Jerusalem and the Ashdod area.

title: Iran missile hits Beit Shemesh area near Jerusalem; ~20 injured, synagogue damaged
description: An Iranian ballistic missile struck the Beit Shemesh area near Jerusalem, injuring nearly 20 people — at least 5 from shrapnel and 8 more from anxiety and trauma. Several homes and a synagogue were damaged. Separately, Iranian munitions impacted buildings in Eilat in southern Israel, prompting emergency searches for trapped persons. The strikes were part of a broader salvo that also hit central Israel.

title: Israel strikes Arak heavy water reactor and Ardakan yellowcake plant
description: Over 50 IAF fighter jets struck the Shahid Khondab Heavy Water Complex near Arak (~250 km southwest of Tehran), capable of producing weapons-grade plutonium, and the Ardakan yellowcake production plant in Yazd Province, which processes uranium ore from the nearby Saghand mine. Ardakan had not been attacked during the June 2025 war. Iran's Atomic Energy Organization confirmed both facilities were hit but reported no casualties or radiation risk. Separately, Israeli strikes hit Iran's Khuzestan and Mobarakeh steel plants. Russia's Rosatom warned of "escalating safety risks" at Bushehr nuclear plant after the third strike near the facility in 10 days.

title: USS Tripoli ARG with 31st MEU arrives in CENTCOM waters from Japan
description: The Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group — USS Tripoli, USS New Orleans, and USS San Diego — arrived in CENTCOM waters carrying the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit (~2,200 Marines and sailors) from Sasebo, Japan. The embarked air wing includes ~20 F-35B Lightning II fighters (VMFA-121), MV-22B Ospreys (VMM-265), and MH-60S Seahawks (HSC-25). The deployment followed Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's approval of a CENTCOM request for reinforcements. An additional ~2,500 Marines from the 11th MEU aboard USS Boxer are en route from California.

title: Israel kills Al Mayadeen and Al Manar journalists in targeted strike in southern Lebanon
description: An Israeli strike on a press-marked vehicle on a road near Jezzi, southern Lebanon, killed three journalists: Al Mayadeen correspondent Fatima Ftouni and her brother/cameraman Mohamed Ftouni, and Al Manar (Hezbollah-owned) correspondent Ali Shoeib. The IDF stated Shoeib "operated within the Hezbollah terrorist organization" and "systematically exposed locations of IDF soldiers." Lebanese President Joseph Aoun condemned the strikes as violating international law and the 1949 Geneva Conventions. A Lebanese army soldier was also killed in a separate Israeli strike the same day.

title: Rubio tells G7 war ends in 2-4 weeks; Vance: goal to 'neuter' Iran
description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told G7 foreign ministers in France that the war is expected to last another 2-4 weeks and that the US can achieve all objectives without ground troops: "We're going to destroy their navy, air force, and significantly destroy their missile launchers so they can never hide behind these things to get a nuclear weapon." He said operations are "on or ahead of schedule." VP JD Vance said the war will continue "a little while longer" to ensure Iran is "neutered for a very, very long time" — a rare acknowledgment that military gains will not be permanent.

title: Trump slams NATO: 'very disappointed,' calls UK carriers 'toys'
description: President Trump called NATO's lack of support in the Iran war a "tremendous mistake," saying: "We will come to their rescue, but they will never come to ours. We don't need them." He described British aircraft carriers as "toys compared to what we have" and questioned future US commitment to the alliance. The UK has not participated militarily in strikes but deployed aircraft in a defensive role to intercept missiles. G7 foreign ministers issued a joint statement calling for "immediate cessation of attacks against civilians and civilian infrastructure."

title: US and Iraq form joint committee to prevent attacks from Iraqi territory
description: The US and Iraq established a High Joint Coordination Committee, holding its first meeting on March 26. Both sides committed to preventing Iraqi territory, airspace, and territorial waters from being used as a launching point for attacks against either Iraqi or US forces and diplomatic missions. The committee aims to keep Iraq outside the regional conflict. Separately, Tehran-backed Kataeb Hezbollah extended its pause on attacks against the US embassy in Baghdad, first announced March 19.

title: Iran launches Op True Promise 4 Wave 83: strikes Ashdod, UAE, Saudi, Kuwait, Bahrain US bases
description: The IRGC announced Operation True Promise 4, Wave 83, employing long-range solid and liquid-fuel missiles, multi-warhead precision systems, and loitering drones. Named targets included storage tanks and an oil depot at Ashdod (Israel); a US military information exchange center; aircraft hangars at Al-Dhafra Air Base (UAE) and Al-Udeiri Air Base (Saudi Arabia); maintenance and transport aircraft at Ali Al-Salem Air Base (Kuwait); and a Patriot system maintenance hangar at Sheikh Isa Base (Bahrain). Iran also fired a fourth salvo at central and southern Israel using cluster bomb warheads; air defenses intercepted most but bomblets scattered over wide areas.

title: Israel strikes Parchin missile complex; 60+ jets, 150+ munitions, 1,000+ Iran targets hit total
description: The IDF struck Parchin, east of Tehran — described as Iran's "most central facility for the production of missiles and defense systems" — along with a warhead casting and filling factory, a ballistic missile engine production site, and a solid-fuel missile component plant. Over 60 IAF fighter jets dropped more than 150 munitions in a single wave. IDF spokesman Brig. Gen. Defrin stated the IDF has now hit more than 1,000 targets in Iran's weapons production industries since the war began and assessed the Parchin and Shahrud strikes have "set Iran back years" in missile production capability; total IDF target hits in Iran have exceeded 15,000.

title: Israel strikes Yazd: Iran's primary naval missile and sea mine production facility
description: The IDF struck what it called Iran's "most central" facility for naval missile and sea mine production in Yazd, approximately 600 km southeast of Tehran. The IDF stated the site handled "planning, development, assembly, and storage of advanced missiles intended for launch from cruise platforms, submarines, and helicopters toward both mobile and stationary maritime targets." The strike was guided by military intelligence and the Navy's intelligence flotilla and is distinct from the overnight Tehran/Parchin strikes which targeted ballistic missile components.

title: Saudi Arabia intercepts 17 drones over Eastern Province, 8 over Riyadh, and 6 ballistic missiles
description: Saudi Arabia's Defence Ministry spokesman Maj. Gen. Turki Al-Malki confirmed interception and destruction of 17 drones over the Eastern Region, 8 drones over the Riyadh region (including two separate waves within a single hour), and the launch of 6 ballistic missiles toward Riyadh — 2 intercepted, 4 falling into the Arabian Gulf or uninhabited areas. A separate interception of 5 drones in the Eastern Province was also confirmed. The Saudi air defense alert system has been active continuously for 27 days since the war began.

title: Pentagon weighs deploying 10,000 additional ground troops to Middle East — WSJ
description: The Wall Street Journal reported the Pentagon is considering deploying up to 10,000 additional ground troops to the Middle East, on top of the ~5,000 Marines of the 31st and 11th MEUs already en route and the 82nd Airborne brigade (~3,000) already ordered to the region. The additional force would include infantry and armored vehicles positioned within striking distance of Iran, including for a potential Kharg Island seizure operation. The 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines) was expected to arrive by March 28. Deputy White House Press Secretary Anna Kelly confirmed: "President Trump always has all military options at his disposal."

title: UAE calls for multinational Hormuz Security Force; drafting UNSC resolution with Bahrain
description: The Financial Times reported that the UAE privately informed the US and Western allies it would deploy its navy to a multinational "Hormuz Security Force" to defend the strait and escort shipping. The UAE and Bahrain are jointly drafting a UNSC resolution to authorize "all necessary means" for shipping protection, though Russian and Chinese vetoes are anticipated. A 22-nation joint statement expressing readiness to contribute has already been issued. Saudi Arabia has not yet publicly committed. The UAE has been struck by more Iranian missiles than any other country in the conflict.

title: Lebanon expels Iran ambassador; Iranian missile explodes over Lebanese airspace — a first
description: Lebanese FM Youssef Raggi declared Iran's ambassador-designate Mohammad Reza Shibani persona non grata, ordering him to leave by March 29, and recalled Lebanon's own ambassador from Tehran. Hours after the announcement, an Iranian Qadr-110 ballistic missile exploded in Lebanese airspace — the first Iranian projectile ever intercepted over Lebanon — with shrapnel falling on Jounieh's Haret al Sakhr neighborhood; no casualties reported. Hezbollah and Amal ministers boycotted Lebanon's March 26 cabinet session in protest, calling the expulsion "reckless and reprehensible." Iranian FM Araghchi called it "regrettable"; Israeli FM Sa'ar called it "justified and necessary."

title: Witkoff confirms 15-point peace framework; Trump: Iran's 'gift' was allowing 8 tankers through Hormuz
description: US special envoy Steve Witkoff confirmed at a Cabinet meeting that Washington presented Iran a "15-point action list that forms the framework for a peace deal," transmitted through Pakistan as intermediary. Trump separately revealed on Fox News that the "present" Iran gave as a good-faith gesture was allowing eight oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz: "They gave me ships. I gave them 10 days." Iran publicly rejected the 15 points as "one-sided" and "extremely maximalist"; a senior Iranian official told Reuters: "No plan for talks appears realistic at this stage."

title: Iran sets 5 conditions to end war; Araghchi: 'We do not want a ceasefire'
description: Iranian FM Araghchi publicly stated Iran's five conditions for ending the war: (1) complete cessation of US-Israeli aggression; (2) guarantees against recurrence; (3) payment of war reparations; (4) comprehensive end to hostilities across all fronts including all resistance groups (Hezbollah, Houthis, Iraqi factions); (5) Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz recognized. Araghchi said: "We do not want a ceasefire" — arguing it would lead to a "vicious cycle" — and characterized US message exchanges as an "admission of defeat" by Washington. Iran insisted the communications are message exchanges, not negotiations.

title: Trump tells aides to 'wrap up' Iran conflict within weeks — WSJ
description: The Wall Street Journal reported Trump has told senior aides he wants to conclude the Iran conflict in the coming weeks. The directive is interpreted as the context driving both the 10-day energy-sector attack pause extension and the push for direct Pakistan-hosted talks. White House officials are publicly maintaining maximum-pressure language while privately signaling urgency for an end-state. The gap between Trump's stated desire for a quick resolution and Iran's five conditions — including war reparations and Hormuz sovereignty — represents a wide opening negotiating distance.

title: IFRC: Iran civilian toll reaches 1,900 dead, 20,000+ injured; 82,000 buildings damaged
description: The International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies confirmed Iran's cumulative civilian toll at 1,900 killed and over 20,000 injured as of March 27, with 82,000 buildings damaged and 180,000 residents affected. Iran's UN Ambassador told the General Assembly that 600 schools had been destroyed or damaged and accused the US and Israel of genocide. The figures represent the first credible externally-verified count of Iranian civilian casualties since the war began February 28.

title: IDF uncovers Hezbollah tunnel beneath church in Khiam, southern Lebanon
description: Israeli ground forces advancing into Khiam, a town 3 km from the Israeli border, discovered a Hezbollah military tunnel running beneath a church on March 27. The IDF published footage of the tunnel as evidence of Hezbollah's systematic use of civilian infrastructure for military purposes. IDF operations in Khiam have been ongoing since ground forces crossed into Lebanon on March 16; Division 162 was deployed this week as the third Israeli division now operating in southern Lebanon.

title: Hezbollah kills two IDF soldiers in Lebanon; rocket kills civilian in Nahariya
description: Sgt. Aviaad Elchanan Volansky was killed by a Hezbollah anti-tank missile in southern Lebanon on March 26. Staff Sgt. Ori Greenberg was killed in a separate firefight the same day. Israeli civilian Uri Peretz was killed when a Hezbollah rocket struck Nahariya, northern Israel. The deaths brought IDF casualties in the Lebanon ground operation to at least a dozen since operations began March 16. Hezbollah has been firing approximately 150 rockets per day — two-thirds at IDF forces in Lebanon, one-third at Israeli territory.

title: IRGC formally declares Hormuz closed to US and Israeli allies' shipping
description: The IRGC issued a formal announcement that the Strait of Hormuz is closed to shipping "to and from ports of allies and supporters of the Israeli-American enemies." Three container ships of various nationalities turned back after receiving IRGC warnings. The UAE called for an international force to reopen the strait. Tanker traffic is down more than 90%, with fewer than 6 ships transiting daily versus the pre-war average of 130+; the DIA assessed Iran could sustain the closure for 1 to 6 months.

title: Israel deploys third division (162nd) to Lebanon; Lebanese officials call it 'an invasion'
description: Israel deployed Division 162 to southern Lebanon, joining two divisions already operating there and expanding the ground operation's buffer zone further north. Lebanon's Culture Minister Ghassan Salamé told Euronews: "An invasion, not a limited operation." Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam announced Lebanon will file a complaint with the UN Security Council. UNICEF reported over 370,000 children displaced in Lebanon and 121 children killed since the conflict began on March 2.

title: Israeli public strong support for war drops from 74% to 50%; IDF warns of manpower shortage
description: An Israel Democracy Institute survey found strong support among Jewish Israelis for the war fell from 74% at the conflict's start to 50% by late March; opposition rose from 4% to 11.5%. Among Arab Israelis, support dropped from 27% to 19%. IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir separately warned the cabinet of growing manpower shortages after 27 days of high-intensity warfare. The polls represent the clearest sign of domestic Israeli pressure on the Netanyahu government.

title: Iran oil revenue hits $139M/day — sole Gulf exporter via Hormuz, Kharg oil infrastructure intact
description: Bloomberg reported Iran earned approximately $139 million per day from crude sales in March, up from $115 million in February, as Iran remains the only major Gulf oil exporter routing shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. Export volume reached ~1.6 million barrels per day through March 23, near pre-war levels, with at least 13 VLCCs loading at Kharg since late February. US and Israeli strikes on Kharg have targeted only military installations — air defenses, naval base, and mine storage — leaving the island's 55 crude storage tanks intact. Iranian Light crude's discount to Brent narrowed to $2.10/barrel, the smallest in nearly a year.

title: Houthis signal full military readiness to enter war; Bab al-Mandab closure threatened
description: Houthi leadership publicly announced full military readiness to enter the conflict if Iran requests, with a commander stating: "We stand fully militarily ready with all options. As for other details having to do with determining zero hour they are left to leadership." Houthi entry would allow closure of the Bab al-Mandab Strait (18 miles wide at its narrowest), severing Red Sea access to the Suez Canal and compounding the Hormuz shutdown. The group has been expanding domestic weapons production and recruiting fighters despite stockpiles depleted during the Gaza campaign.

title: IDF strikes Hezbollah paramedic center in Hebbariye; 7 killed
description: An Israeli airstrike hit a paramedic center affiliated with Hezbollah in Hebbariye, southern Lebanon, killing 7 people. The IDF stated those killed were Hezbollah militants using the facility; Hezbollah and Lebanese health officials disputed this, saying those killed were emergency responders. Lebanese Health Ministry noted 42 health workers have been killed in Lebanon since March 2. Lebanon's PM Salam's office condemned the strike.

title: Iran fires two missile barrages at central Israel; 2 lightly wounded in Ramat Gan
description: Iran launched two separate missile rounds targeting central Israel on March 27. Magen David Adom treated 6 people in total — 4 wounded while running to shelters and 2 lightly injured in Ramat Gan. At least one salvo included a cluster bomb warhead. Iron Dome and Israel's layered air defenses intercepted the majority of incoming projectiles.

title: Israel strikes dozens of weapon factories and ballistic missile component sites in Tehran overnight
description: The IDF launched a 'wide-scale wave of strikes' on Tehran overnight March 26-27, targeting dozens of weapon manufacturing facilities, a military base storing anti-aircraft systems, a production site for key ballistic missile components, a battery production facility, and an IRGC weapons production complex. Israel also struck ballistic missile launch sites and targeted Iranian soldiers described as preparing to fire on Israel. The US has fired over 850 Tomahawk cruise missiles in four weeks and has destroyed approximately two-thirds of Iran's missile and drone production facilities.

title: Kuwait arrests 6 Hezbollah-linked operatives planning assassinations inside Kuwait
description: Kuwaiti authorities arrested six individuals linked to Hezbollah who were allegedly planning assassination operations inside Kuwait. The arrests came amid Kuwait's heightened security posture following repeated Iranian drone and missile strikes on Kuwaiti territory during the war. Kuwait's interior ministry did not specify the intended targets. The operation signals growing domestic security threats across Gulf states as Iran-backed networks activate amid the broader war.

title: Israel orders all residents south of Lebanon's Zahrani River to evacuate; ground ops expand north
description: The Israeli military issued displacement orders for all residents of southern Lebanon south of the Zahrani River — approximately 50km (31 miles) north of the Israeli border, far beyond the Litani line announced days earlier. The orders cover a substantial portion of Lebanese territory and signal a major northward expansion of Israel's ground operation. Lebanese Health Ministry reported over 1,100 killed and 3,229 wounded in Israeli operations since March 2. The expansion came alongside reports that Israel is 'speeding up' its targeting in Iran over 48 hours in anticipation of a potential ceasefire.

title: Iran parliament drafts Hormuz transit toll law; at least 2 ships paid ~$2M each in yuan
description: Iran's parliament is formalizing a legal framework to charge vessels for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Lawmaker Mohammadreza Rezaei Kouchi stated: 'We provide its security, and it is natural that ships and oil tankers should pay such fees.' Lloyd's List reported that at least 26 vessels have already transited under an IRGC-run 'toll booth' vetting system, with at least two ships paying approximately $2 million each, settled in Chinese yuan. Shipping operators have characterized the arrangement as extortion; Columbia University's Karen Young said Gulf states 'are not going to accept and not going to tolerate' a Hormuz toll regime.

title: EU's Kallas: Russia supplying Iran intelligence to kill Americans; UK cites 'hidden hand of Putin'
description: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas, at the G7 foreign ministers' meeting in France, stated: 'We see that Russia is helping Iran with intelligence to target Americans, to kill Americans, and Russia is also supporting Iran now with the drones.' She urged Washington to pressure Moscow or the Middle East war would not end. UK Defense Secretary John Healey separately cited the 'hidden hand of Putin' and described a Russia-Iran 'axis of aggression.' Western intelligence sources told Reuters that Russia has supplied Iran satellite imagery including locations of US warships, and has helped Iran upgrade its drones.

title: Iran fires missiles and drones at UAE, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Bahrain; UAE debris kills 2
description: Iran launched a fresh wave of missiles and drones across four Gulf states. In the UAE, an intercepted ballistic missile's debris killed 2 and injured 3, raising the country's war death toll to 11. Saudi Arabia intercepted multiple drones heading toward its eastern oil-producing region. Bahrain reported a fire in Muharraq caused by Iranian attack. Kuwait reported loud explosions from interceptions across the country. Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar, and Jordan issued a joint condemnation of Iran's attacks and called on Iraq to prevent Iran-backed groups from launching strikes from its territory.

title: Pakistan FM Dar publicly confirms indirect US-Iran talks via message relay; Turkey and Egypt also involved
description: Pakistani Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar confirmed on social media: 'US-Iran indirect talks are taking place through messages being relayed by Pakistan,' adding Turkey and Egypt were 'extending their support to this initiative.' An unnamed official in Islamabad told NPR that Pakistan's interior minister held a secret meeting that day with Iran's ambassador. Mediators are pushing for in-person US-Iran talks in Pakistan, according to Egyptian and Pakistani officials. Iran's foreign ministry maintained these are message exchanges only, not formal negotiations.

title: Trump extends Iran energy strike pause 10 days to April 6, cites 'Iranian government request'
description: President Trump posted on Truth Social: 'As per Iranian Government request, please let this statement serve to represent that I am pausing the period of Energy Plant destruction by 10 Days to Monday, April 6, 2026, at 8 P.M., Eastern Time.' He added talks are 'going very well.' The extension is Trump's second deadline delay since threatening to destroy Iran's power plants. Steve Witkoff told a Cabinet meeting: 'If we can convince Iran that this is the inflection point with no good alternatives for them, other than more death and destruction, we have strong signs that this is a possibility.' Human rights organizations warned strikes on power plants could constitute war crimes.

title: Israel kills IRGC Navy commander Tangsiri and top naval staff at Bandar Abbas
description: Israeli forces killed IRGC Navy commander Admiral Alireza Tangsiri and 'senior officers of the naval command' in a strike on Bandar Abbas. Defense Minister Katz stated: 'The man who was directly responsible for the terrorist operation of mining and blocking the Strait of Hormuz was blown up and eliminated.' US CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper confirmed the death, adding that US forces have now destroyed approximately 92% of Iran's large naval vessels since the war began. Netanyahu said Tangsiri had 'a great deal of blood on his hands' and was the person 'who led the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.'

title: Trump postpones planned China state visit to mid-May due to ongoing Iran war
description: The White House confirmed Trump's scheduled visit to China has been pushed back to mid-May, citing the ongoing Iran conflict as the reason for the delay. The postponement comes as Wang Yi, China's top diplomat, has been calling regional counterparts including Araghchi urging diplomacy and stating 'talking is always better than fighting.' China remains one of the five nations granted Hormuz passage by Iran, and Beijing has avoided taking sides while quietly pressing for a negotiated exit from the conflict.

title: Trump at Republican dinner: Iran 'negotiating but afraid to say it'; names Vance, Rubio, Witkoff, Kushner as deal team
description: At a dinner for Republican members of Congress, Trump said of Iran: 'They are negotiating, by the way, and they want to make a deal so badly. But they're afraid to say it, because they figure they'll be killed by their own people.' He named VP JD Vance, Secretary of State Marco Rubio, envoy Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner as active participants in the talks — the first public naming of the full US team. Trump also revealed Iran had offered a 'prize' tied to oil, gas, and the Strait of Hormuz. He referred to the conflict as a 'military operation' rather than a 'war,' saying: 'They don't like the word war, because you're supposed to get approval.'

title: Israel strikes submarine development center in Isfahan alongside multiple Tehran strike waves
description: Israel targeted an Iranian submarine development center in Isfahan as part of a broader strike campaign that also completed 'several waves of airstrikes on Tehran.' The Isfahan target — part of Iran's naval-industrial complex developing advanced submarines — represents Israel's continued effort to destroy future force-projection capabilities, not just current missile stocks. Israel's UN Ambassador Danon stated military operations would continue 'until Iran's nuclear and missile capabilities are fully eliminated,' underscoring that Israel views the war as a disarmament campaign with no defined endpoint.

title: White House: Pakistan talks are 'speculation' — but confirms Iran talks 'productive'
description: Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt told reporters that reports of Vance flying to Pakistan for talks with Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf are 'speculation' and 'nothing is official unless announced by the White House.' She simultaneously confirmed 'talks continue and they are productive.' When asked about Vance's role specifically, she deflected without denying it. She also warned: 'President Trump does not bluff and he is prepared to unleash hell — Iran should not miscalculate again.' The mixed messaging reflects internal disagreement over whether to publicize the diplomatic track.

title: Araghchi publicly denies any negotiations: 'We do not engage in talks and have no plans to'
description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi appeared on state television and flatly denied that Iran is in any talks to end the war: 'Iran has not engaged in talks to end the war and does not plan on any negotiations.' He called the US 15-point plan 'extremely maximalist and unreasonable.' In a pointed swipe at Trump, he said: 'Didn't the Americans say unconditional surrender? The fact that they are now talking about negotiation is an admission of defeat.' He acknowledged 'exchange of messages through friendly countries' but insisted this does not constitute negotiations.

title: US-Israel rift: Israel alarmed Trump may declare unilateral ceasefire without Israeli consent
description: Two Israeli government sources told reporters that Israel is alarmed by the possibility that the Trump administration could unilaterally announce a one-month ceasefire in Iran to facilitate negotiations — without Israeli agreement. Israel's UN Ambassador Danny Danon stated publicly: 'Israel is not part of US-Iran talks. Our military operations will continue until Iran's nuclear and missile capabilities are fully eliminated.' The tension signals a potential open rift between Washington and Jerusalem over how and when the war ends.

title: Iran reinforcing Kharg Island defenses in anticipation of US ground assault
description: CNN reported that Iran has been deploying additional IRGC personnel, laying defensive traps, and repositioning air defense systems on Kharg Island in anticipation of a US amphibious or airborne assault. One senior IRGC official stated: 'We are closely monitoring all US movements, especially troop deployments. Do not test our resolve.' Gulf allies are privately urging Washington not to attempt a Kharg ground operation, warning of high US casualties and Iranian retaliation across regional infrastructure. The DIA has assessed Iran could keep the Strait closed for 1–6 months even without Kharg.

title: Israel approves raising military reserves to 400,000; plans 10-20 mile Lebanon buffer zone
description: Israel's cabinet approved raising the reserve soldier mobilization cap from 280,000 to 400,000. PM Netanyahu announced Israel would 'expand' its presence in southern Lebanon by 10–20 miles, describing it as a 'larger buffer zone using the Gaza model.' Defense Minister Katz confirmed the focus on controlling the Litani River line. Lebanon's total death toll stands at 1,072+, wounded 2,966+, displaced 1.2 million+. Finance Minister Smotrich publicly called for annexation of occupied Lebanese territory.

title: Lebanon expels Iranian ambassador, effective March 29; unprecedented break with Tehran
description: The Lebanese government announced it is expelling Iran's ambassador, giving him until Sunday March 29 to leave the country. PM Nawaf Salam stated the decision reflects Iran's role in directing Hezbollah to fight a war 'imposed on Lebanon.' Israel praised the move; Hezbollah condemned it. The Lebanese government has also formally banned Hezbollah military operations and called on the group to place its weapons under state authority — the most direct confrontation between the Beirut government and Hezbollah in recent memory.

title: US kills 15 PMF fighters in Iraq airstrike; Iraq grants PMF formal right to retaliate
description: A US airstrike at a PMF base in Habbaniyah, Al Anbar Governorate killed 15 fighters from Iran-backed Popular Mobilization Forces, including a local commander. Iraq's federal government subsequently granted Iran-backed PMF groups formal legal authority to respond to US attacks on Iraqi soil. The Islamic Resistance in Iraq claimed 23 separate operations against US bases in the 24 hours ending March 25. Iraq had already summoned the US chargé d'affaires and filed a formal protest over the strike.

title: Iran grants selective Hormuz passage to China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan — 2,000 ships still stranded
description: FM Araghchi confirmed Iran has formally granted Hormuz passage only to vessels from five 'friendly' nations: China, Russia, India, Iraq, and Pakistan. All other shipping must coordinate with Iranian naval authorities or face interception. One container vessel (SELEN) was expelled for violating Iran's rules. Approximately 2,000 vessels and 20,000 seafarers remain stranded in or near the Strait. QatarEnergy, Shell, Kuwait Petroleum, and Bapco have invoked force majeure. Brent crude trades around $100–110/barrel — down from a $126 peak but ~40% above prewar levels.

title: UN Secretary-General: conflict 'totally out of control'; $63 billion in Arab economic losses
description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres stated the fighting 'has broken past limits even leaders thought imaginable' and warned the conflict is 'totally out of control.' The UN separately assessed cumulative economic losses across the Arab region at $63 billion. France's military chief was reported to be preparing talks with regional navies to restore maritime navigation through the Strait of Hormuz. The UN Security Council has been unable to pass any resolution due to US and Russian vetoes on competing texts.

title: US public opposition to war reaches 59–61% across three major polls
description: Three simultaneous polls found strong American public opposition to the Iran war: Pew Research (59% wrong decision vs. 38%), Quinnipiac (54% oppose vs. 39% support), and Reuters/Ipsos (61% disapprove vs. 35% approve). The Quinnipiac breakdown showed 92% of Democrats, 64% of independents, and 14% of Republicans opposed. The polls represent the clearest domestic political pressure on the Trump administration to pursue a negotiated exit from the conflict.

title: Iran formally rejects US 15-point ceasefire plan; brands terms 'excessive'
description: Iran officially rejected the US 15-point ceasefire proposal transmitted via Pakistani intermediaries. Iranian state broadcaster Press TV, citing a senior political-security official, called the terms 'excessive' and a 'ploy.' Iran formally stated it had responded 'negatively' and that the war would end 'when Iran decides it should end, not when Trump envisions its conclusion.' The rejection came alongside a formal Iranian five-point counter-proposal that included Hormuz sovereignty, war reparations, and guarantees against future strikes — all considered non-starters by Washington.

title: Iran partially opens Strait of Hormuz to 'non-hostile' vessels — only 5 ships transit vs 120/day prewar
description: Iran's UN mission announced that vessels not associated with the US, Israel, or their supporters may transit the Strait of Hormuz 'in coordination with competent Iranian authorities.' Despite the announcement, only 5 ships were tracked transiting versus a prewar average of 120 per day, with an estimated 400 vessels still anchored outside the strait. The partial concession — widely interpreted as the 'oil and gas present' Trump had teased publicly — stopped well short of restoring normal shipping. Analysts noted Iranian naval vessels are still shadowing transiting ships.

title: Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr named Iran's new National Security Council chief, replacing killed Larijani
description: President Pezeshkian, with approval from new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, appointed IRGC veteran Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council, replacing Ali Larijani who was killed in an Israeli strike. Zolghadr served as IRGC Joint Staff chief for eight years and deputy commander-in-chief for another eight. He is sanctioned by the US, UK, and EU. Analysts say his appointment militarizes Iran's security decision-making — any negotiated outcome will now require the sign-off of a career IRGC commander with deep hostility toward Washington.

title: US and Israel grant temporary diplomatic immunity to Araghchi and Qalibaf for negotiation window
description: Per Israeli Channel 14, the US and Israel jointly granted temporary immunity from targeting to Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, valid for the duration of the current five-day diplomatic pause. The move is significant: Israel's Defense Minister had previously declared all Islamic Republic leaders legitimate targets. The immunity grants are tied directly to the negotiation window, with the implicit threat that they expire if talks collapse.

title: Pentagon formally confirms 82nd Airborne deployment — 2,000–3,000 paratroopers to CENTCOM
description: The Pentagon officially confirmed the deployment of the 82nd Airborne Division HQ, enablers, and the 1st Brigade Combat Team to CENTCOM's area of responsibility. Between 2,000 and 3,000 paratroopers are affected, commanded by Maj. Gen. Brandon Tegtmeier. Possible missions include seizure of Kharg Island, which handles 90% of Iran's remaining oil exports. The deployment adds to 50,000 US troops already in the region plus two Marine Expeditionary Units.

title: Israel announces 'security zone' controlling all southern Lebanon to the Litani River
description: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz publicly announced Israel would establish a security zone controlling all of southern Lebanon up to the Litani River — roughly 10% of Lebanese territory. He stated all five bridges over the Litani had been destroyed by Israeli forces to cut off supply and retreat routes. Lebanon's total death toll has surpassed 1,072, with 1.2 million displaced. Far-right Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich publicly called for annexation of the occupied zone.

title: Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem vows to fight 'without limits'; rejects any negotiations
description: Hezbollah Secretary-General Naim Qassem called for Lebanese national unity against the Israeli advance and vowed to fight 'without limits.' He explicitly rejected any negotiations with Israel while bombardment continues, calling the Israeli occupation push an 'existential threat to Lebanon.' Hezbollah struck Israeli positions at Naqura and Qawzah with over 100 rockets. Lebanese PM Salam stated the IRGC is directing Hezbollah's operations from Lebanese territory.

title: Kim Jong Un: Iran war proves nuclear weapons 'irreversible necessity'; North Korea reportedly arming Iran
description: Kim Jong Un addressed North Korea's Supreme People's Assembly and declared the country's nuclear-armed status 'irreversible,' explicitly citing US actions in the Iran war as proof that only nuclear weapons guarantee regime survival. North Korea is also reportedly supplying weapons to Iran via covert channels — analysts describe a 'rogue strategic pipeline' from Pyongyang to Tehran delivering munitions that extend Iran's missile campaign. The statements signal a broader proliferation effect from the conflict.

title: Houthis reinforcing Red Sea positions; holding fire awaiting Tehran signal
description: Despite vocal pledges of solidarity with Iran, Houthis have fired no Red Sea missiles since the war began. Analysts cite a combination of a quiet US-Saudi arrangement and an explicit instruction from Tehran to conserve Houthi strike capacity. New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's hints at 'opening new fronts' are widely read as a signal to Houthis to be ready. Houthis are reinforcing their Red Sea coastal positions and expanding weapons production. If ordered to attack, the re-engagement would threaten 15% of global container shipping.

title: Pentagon requests $200 billion supplemental war budget; Hegseth: 'It takes money to kill bad guys'
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed the Pentagon is seeking a $200 billion supplemental appropriation from Congress for the Iran war — four times early estimates and in addition to a record $1.5 trillion FY2027 base request. Hegseth justified the figure with the quote 'It takes money to kill bad guys.' Defense contractors are scaling to meet surge demand: Raytheon's Patriot interceptor production is ramping from 600 to 2,000/year, THAAD from 96 to 400/year, and Lockheed Martin's PrSM precision missiles from 400 to 1,600/year.

title: Second strike hits Bushehr NPP; IAEA confirms 350m from reactor, Rosatom warns of fuel risk
description: A second projectile struck the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant compound at 9:08 p.m. local time. The IAEA confirmed 'a structure 350 metres from the reactor was hit and destroyed.' No casualties or reactor damage reported. Russian state nuclear operator Rosatom issued a stark warning: the active reactor holds 72 tons of nuclear fuel and 210 tons of spent fuel — a potential regional-scale radiation disaster. Rosatom announced it is preparing a third evacuation of its ~480 remaining personnel. The back-to-back strikes on the plant represent the most dangerous nuclear threshold crossed since the war began.

title: Israel strikes IRGC intelligence HQ and Ministry of Intelligence in Tehran
description: Israel struck two IRGC intelligence headquarters and Iran's Ministry of Intelligence building in Tehran, as part of a wave targeting over 50 sites including ballistic missile storage and launch facilities. The US separately struck a turbine engine production facility in Qom province used for IRGC drone and aircraft components. Targeting Iran's domestic intelligence apparatus marks an escalation beyond military infrastructure toward the Iranian security state itself.

title: South Tehran strike kills 12, wounds 28
description: A US-Israeli strike on south Tehran killed at least 12 people and wounded 28 others. The strike targeted IRGC facilities embedded in a residential district. The attack came hours after Trump publicly claimed ceasefire talks were progressing — drawing sharp criticism from Iran, which called simultaneous diplomacy and strikes 'a contradiction that exposes US bad faith.'

title: Iranian drones hit Kuwait airport fuel depot; fourth attack on Kuwait since war began
description: Iranian drones struck a fuel storage tank at Kuwait International Airport, igniting a fire. Kuwait's Civil Aviation Authority confirmed limited damage and no casualties. Kuwait's army intercepted 6 drones and 5 additional UAVs in the same wave. The IRGC stated it targeted 'military bases hosting US forces in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain.' This is the fourth Iranian drone strike on Kuwait's airport since the war began, escalating pressure on Gulf states providing logistical support to US operations.

title: Iran military mocks ceasefire talks; demands US close all Gulf bases as condition
description: Iran's military spokesperson Lt. Col. Ebrahim Zolfaghari publicly mocked US ceasefire efforts, saying Americans were 'only negotiating with themselves.' Iran's formal counter-demands include: closure of all US military bases in the Gulf, financial compensation for war damages, end to Israel's anti-Hezbollah campaign, and Iran levying fees on Hormuz shipping. The public position directly contradicts a private signal from an Iranian source indicating Tehran is 'willing to listen' to suitable proposals.

title: US lifts Iranian oil sanctions for 30 days — 140 million barrels unlocked
description: The Trump administration enacted a temporary 30-day lifting of Iranian oil sanctions to contain energy prices. Approximately 140 million barrels of Iranian oil were unlocked for export. Additional emergency measures included a Jones Act suspension, temporary Russian oil sanctions relief, and coordinated IEA strategic reserve releases. Brent crude had peaked above $126/barrel before the announcement. The sanction relief marked the first significant economic concession by the US since the war began.

title: US six-condition ceasefire framework delivered to Iran via Egypt, Qatar and UK
description: The United States communicated a formal six-condition ceasefire proposal to Iran through intermediaries including Egypt, Qatar, and the UK. Conditions include: external monitoring of nuclear centrifuges, a cap on Iran's ballistic missile inventory at 1,000 units, termination of support for Hezbollah, Houthis, and Hamas, and withdrawal of IRGC personnel from Lebanon and Syria. Iran has not publicly responded but confirmed receipt via the Foreign Ministry.

title: High-level US–Iran talks targeted for March 26 — Vance and Ghalibaf potentially involved
description: The US and regional mediators are working to arrange high-level talks as early as March 26, potentially between VP JD Vance and Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, or at a lower level between Foreign Minister Araghchi and US envoys Kushner/Witkoff. Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, Oman, and Qatar are all serving as relay channels. Iran's Supreme National Security Council has not yet approved the meeting. Israel told Washington it was 'surprised things were moving that fast.'

title: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant struck; AEOI warns of 'irreversible consequences'
description: Iran's Atomic Energy Organization confirmed a projectile struck the grounds of the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant — the first time an operational civilian nuclear reactor has been targeted in the conflict. Initial assessments indicated no damage to nuclear systems and no radiation release, but the AEOI warned of 'dangerous and irreversible consequences' of attacking peaceful nuclear facilities. Russian FM Lavrov separately condemned the strikes as 'extremely dangerous.' The attack marks a significant escalation threshold.

title: Russia condemns Bushehr strikes as 'extremely dangerous'; urges Iran to end hostilities
description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov called his Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi and delivered a dual message: condemning strikes near Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant as 'extremely dangerous and unacceptable,' while also urging Iran to de-escalate and end hostilities. The call reflects Russia's increasingly uncomfortable position — sympathetic to Iran but alarmed by nuclear escalation risks and unwilling to be drawn into direct confrontation with the US.

title: Trump claims Iran has agreed to never develop nuclear weapons
description: Speaking at an Oval Office ceremony, President Trump claimed that Iran had agreed it 'will never have a nuclear weapon,' and said VP Vance, Rubio, Kushner, and Witkoff are all actively 'in negotiations' with Tehran. Iran's Foreign Ministry immediately denied the claim, calling it 'psychological warfare.' Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf called Trump's statements 'fake news intended to manipulate financial markets.' Despite the contradiction, oil prices dropped sharply on the announcement.

title: Israel fears premature ceasefire; IDF requests weeks more of fighting
description: Israeli officials expressed deep skepticism and disappointment at Trump's sudden ceasefire push. IDF senior leadership told their US counterparts they need 'several more weeks' of fighting to achieve stated war objectives including the destruction of Iran's nuclear infrastructure and degrading IRGC capabilities. Israel was briefed on the US 15-point plan but doubts Iran will accept the conditions. Officials noted Israel was 'surprised at how fast things were moving' when Trump revealed the contacts.

title: Missile with 100kg warhead strikes central Tel Aviv — not intercepted
description: A ballistic missile carrying a 100-kilogram warhead struck a street in central Tel Aviv, blowing out windows across multiple apartment blocks and wounding four people. Israeli air defenses failed to intercept the missile. The strike was part of Iran's ongoing Operation True Promise 4. It follows the earlier David's Sling failure on March 22 and marks the second significant interception failure within 48 hours, raising questions about the sustainability of Israel's multi-layered air defense.

title: Hezbollah rocket kills woman in northern Israel
description: A Hezbollah rocket attack on northern Israel killed one woman and wounded two others. Hezbollah continues to fire rockets and anti-tank missiles into northern Israel despite Israeli airstrikes on Beirut's southern suburbs and the severing of supply routes. Lebanon's own government has called on Hezbollah to cease military operations and surrender its weapons to the state.

title: Iraq summons US and Iranian envoys; PM condemns 'systematic aggression'
description: The Iraqi government summoned both the US chargé d'affaires and the Iranian ambassador to deliver formal notes of protest over the deadly PMF airstrike in Anbar province and Iranian attacks on Iraqi territory. PM Mohammed al-Sudani condemned the US strikes as 'systematic aggression' and convened the National Security Council, which invoked Iraq's right to self-defense. Iraq finds itself caught between its treaty obligations to the US and its close ties to Iran-aligned groups embedded in its armed forces.

title: Pentagon prepares 82nd Airborne deployment to Middle East despite ceasefire signals
description: Despite Trump's diplomatic overtures and five-day pause on energy infrastructure strikes, the Pentagon is preparing to deploy thousands of troops from the elite 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East. The deployment — exact location and timing undisclosed — contrasts sharply with the administration's simultaneous ceasefire diplomacy. Defense Secretary Hegseth was reported to be 'quite disappointed' by the negotiation track, signaling deep internal divisions in the US national security establishment over the war's direction.

title: US casualties reach 290 wounded, 13 killed across seven countries
description: CENTCOM spokesperson Navy Capt. Tim Hawkins confirmed 290 US service members have been wounded and 13 killed in action since the start of Operation Epic Fury. Casualties are spread across Bahrain, Iraq, Israel, Jordan, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and UAE. Most injuries are attributed to Iranian drone and missile attacks on bases hosting US personnel. Ten service members remain seriously wounded. The milestone comes as the US simultaneously pursues ceasefire negotiations.

title: IRGC's 78th wave targets Dimona, Tel Aviv and Eilat with Emad and Qadr missiles
description: Iran's IRGC announced its 78th wave of retaliatory strikes under Operation True Promise 4, targeting the Dimona nuclear research area, Tel Aviv, and Eilat using Emad and multi-warhead Qadr ballistic missile systems alongside attack drones. This was the second deliberate targeting of Dimona, Iran's largest missile salvo of the conflict, and the first use of Qadr multi-warhead systems in the conflict. Israeli air defenses intercepted most projectiles but several breached coverage.

title: IDF strikes Hezbollah in Beirut southern suburbs; pursuing 'Rafah model' for Lebanon
description: The Israeli Air Force struck Hezbollah command infrastructure in Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahieh), issuing evacuation warnings beforehand. Israeli officials are reported to be pursuing a 'Rafah model' approach — systematically clearing Hezbollah from southern Lebanon district by district, as was done in Rafah during the 2024 Gaza war. Lebanese authorities report over 1,100 killed in Israeli Lebanon strikes since March 2.

title: Iran rejects Trump's peace claims as 'fake news'; IRGC threatens Tel Aviv strike
description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard called Trump a 'deceitful American president' and labelled his claims of ongoing peace negotiations 'fake news' and a 'big lie.' IRGC-linked sources warned 'special plans are arranged tonight for Tel Aviv and some regional allies of the US and Israel, which will completely remove any hope of negotiation.' Iranian officials accused the US of fabricating talks to manipulate global oil and financial markets and buy time as more US troops deploy to the region.

title: Brent crude plunges 7%+ to ~$104/barrel on Trump's 5-day pause announcement
description: Oil markets reacted sharply to Trump's postponement of Iranian energy infrastructure strikes. Brent crude fell more than 7% to around $104/barrel — touching below $99 at its intraday low, a drop of over 13% from the day's peak of $114. WTI fell 6.9% to $91.4/barrel. Despite the drop, prices remain over 40% above the pre-war level of $72.48. Asian equity markets also rallied: Japan's Nikkei up 0.9%, South Korea's Kospi up 1.1%, and Hong Kong's Hang Seng up 1.4%.

title: Pakistan offers Iran-US mediation; Netanyahu says military gains can convert to a deal
description: Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif spoke with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, pledging to play 'a constructive role in advancing peace.' Israeli PM Netanyahu, after speaking with Trump, said the US president believes major military gains against Iran can be converted into a negotiated agreement protecting Israel's vital interests — though Israel would continue strikes in both Iran and Lebanon in the meantime. US envoys Witkoff and Kushner were reported to be in contact with Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf. Netanyahu also said Israel had recently killed two Iranian nuclear scientists.

title: Iran threatens complete Hormuz closure if power plants hit
description: Iran's Supreme National Security Council issued a formal warning that any strike on Iranian power infrastructure would trigger a complete and permanent closure of the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian state media also disputed Trump's claim of 'productive' ceasefire talks, saying no direct or indirect negotiations had taken place and accusing the US of 'psychological warfare.'

title: Trump pauses Iran energy strikes for 5 days as ceasefire talks begin in Geneva
description: President Trump postponed threatened strikes on Iranian power plants for five days, citing 'very good and productive conversations' over the past two days. Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner participated in talks with a senior Iranian official in Geneva. Trump claimed 'major points of agreement' exist but warned 'otherwise we'll just keep bombing our little hearts out.'

title: Turkey's FM Fidan leads intensive ceasefire diplomacy with 12+ calls to counterparts
description: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan conducted more than a dozen phone calls with regional and global counterparts over 48 hours, with 'steps aimed at stopping the war' discussed on each call. Ankara has emerged as one of the few parties with channels to both Washington and Tehran, and Trump confirmed Qatar is being considered as the preferred mediation venue.

title: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants within 48 hours
description: President Trump posted on Truth Social threatening to 'completely and totally obliterate' all Iranian power generation facilities unless Iran ceased military operations within 48 hours, raising fears of a catastrophic infrastructure war. Israeli Defence Minister Katz separately said the US and Israel would 'significantly increase the intensity of strikes.'

title: Israel bombs key bridges in southern Lebanon, severing Hezbollah supply routes
description: Israeli Air Force conducted strikes on six major road bridges in southern Lebanon, severing key supply routes used by Hezbollah to move fighters and materiel. The strikes coincide with a broader ground offensive. Lebanese authorities report more than 1,029 killed in Israeli strikes in Lebanon since March 2.

title: Iranian missiles strike near Dimona nuclear facility; at least 180 wounded
description: Iran launched a salvo of ballistic missiles targeting Israel's Negev near the Dimona nuclear research complex and the city of Arad, wounding at least 180 people. Two missiles penetrated defences after Israel's David's Sling interceptor system failed — a system recently upgraded and deployed to conserve Arrow stocks. An initial inquiry also found the US THAAD system missed a missile over Arad. The IDF said the two interception failures were unrelated coincidences, not a systemic breakdown. Iran's IRGC claimed it deliberately targeted Dimona in retaliation for Israeli strikes on Natanz.

title: Lebanese PM Salam accuses IRGC of commanding Hezbollah from Lebanon; demands weapons surrender
description: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam stated publicly that Iran's Revolutionary Guards are present in Lebanon and 'managing the military operation' on behalf of Hezbollah, citing an IRGC-linked drone that struck a British base in Cyprus as evidence. Salam declared 'Hezbollah's threats do not intimidate us' and said Beirut had taken the unprecedented step of banning Hezbollah military activities and calling on the group to hand its weapons over to the Lebanese state. He said the government is working to 'regain control over the decision to go to war.'

title: 22-nation maritime coalition pledges to secure Strait of Hormuz
description: A US-led maritime coalition announced that 22 nations had committed naval assets to protect freedom of navigation through the Strait of Hormuz, signaling broad international resolve to reopen the waterway. Bahrain confirmed it has destroyed 143 missiles and 242 drones fired by Iran since the war began on February 28.

title: Iran attacks Diego Garcia US base at record 3,800 km range
description: Iran fired a barrage of long-range ballistic missiles at the US Diego Garcia military base in the Indian Ocean, the farthest-range strike ever attempted by Iran. The attack demonstrated a dramatic expansion of Iran's operational reach and raised alarm among US military planners about the vulnerability of previously safe rear-area facilities.

title: Iran threatens UAE with 'crushing blows' over US base access
description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard issued an explicit threat against the United Arab Emirates, warning of 'crushing retaliatory blows' if UAE continued to allow US forces to use Al Dhafra Air Base for strike operations against Iran. The warning came despite UAE having already suffered Iranian missile and drone attacks since February 28.

title: Saudi Arabia expels Iran's military attaché and four embassy staff over repeated attacks
description: Saudi Arabia declared Iran's military attaché, assistant military attaché, and three additional embassy staff members persona non grata, giving them 24 hours to leave the kingdom. Riyadh cited repeated Iranian attacks on Saudi sovereignty, civilian infrastructure, and economic interests as violations of international law, the 2023 Beijing normalization agreement, and UNSC Resolution 2817. Qatar took a similar step, expelling Iranian military and security attachés following the strike on Ras Laffan. The Saudi foreign minister warned all trust with Tehran had been shattered and reserved the right to act militarily against further Iranian aggression.

title: Trump hints at 'winding down' operations even as 3 warships and 2,500 Marines deploy
description: President Trump said the US is considering 'winding down' military operations, calling Iran 'finished' from a military standpoint. However, the same day his administration deployed three additional warships and 2,500 Marines to the region, bringing total US military personnel deployed for the war to approximately 50,000.

title: Iranian drones hit Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi refinery
description: A swarm of Iranian drones struck the Mina al-Ahmadi oil refinery in Kuwait, one of the largest in the Middle East, causing fires that burned for several hours and temporarily halting operations. The strike sends oil futures sharply higher and deepens the Gulf region's energy crisis.

title: IRGC spokesman Ali Mohammad Naeini eliminated in Israeli airstrike
description: IRGC public affairs spokesman Brigadier General Ali Mohammad Naeini was killed in a precision airstrike in Tehran attributed to the Israeli Air Force. His death marks the latest in a string of senior Iranian military and government figures eliminated since Operation Epic Fury began on February 28.

title: Israel strikes Iranian naval targets in Caspian Sea for first time in history
description: Israeli forces conducted an unprecedented operation striking Iranian naval vessels and port facilities on the Caspian Sea shore, marking the first-ever Israeli military action in that body of water and signaling a dramatic widening of the conflict's geographic scope beyond the Persian Gulf and Middle East.

title: Iran attacks Haifa oil refinery, disrupting operations
description: Iranian ballistic missiles struck Israel's Haifa oil refinery complex, causing significant damage to processing units and forcing a temporary shutdown. Fires burned for hours before being brought under control. The strike is part of a sustained Iranian campaign targeting Israeli economic infrastructure.

title: Brent crude hits $115/barrel amid ongoing Hormuz disruption
description: Global oil prices climbed back to $115 per barrel for Brent crude as sustained strikes on Gulf energy infrastructure and the continued effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz kept markets on edge. The IEA has called this the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market.

title: G7+ nations issue joint statement condemning Iran's Hormuz closure and commercial shipping attacks
description: The leaders of the UK, France, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, Japan, Canada, South Korea, New Zealand, Denmark, Norway, Sweden, Finland, Australia, and others signed a joint statement condemning 'in the strongest terms' Iran's attacks on unarmed commercial vessels, strikes on oil and gas infrastructure, and the de facto closure of the Strait of Hormuz. They called on Iran to immediately cease mine-laying, drone and missile attacks, and all attempts to block the strait, and to comply with UN Security Council Resolution 2817. Hormuz transits had fallen by approximately 95% from peacetime levels, with over 200 vessels anchored outside the strait.

title: Iran launches record 19 missile attack waves against Israel in a single day
description: Israeli air defenses recorded 19 separate Iranian ballistic missile attack waves in a single day on March 19 — the highest single-day figure of the war. Six waves targeted the Tel Aviv area, six targeted northern Israel, five aimed at Jerusalem, and two hit southern Israel. The barrage was seen as Iran's largest coordinated strike since February 28.

title: Iranian intelligence minister Esmaeil Khatib killed in precision strike near Qom
description: Iranian Minister of Intelligence Esmaeil Khatib was killed in a targeted strike attributed to Israel on a convoy near the city of Qom. His death represents the highest-ranking Iranian government official eliminated since Ali Khamenei's assassination on February 28, deepening the decapitation of Iran's state apparatus.

title: Iran retaliates against Qatar's LNG facility following South Pars strike
description: In retaliation for Israeli strikes on South Pars, Iran launched a missile and drone strike against Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG facility, the world's largest single-site natural gas complex, causing partial damage and triggering an emergency shutdown. Qatar evacuated non-essential personnel from the facility.

title: Israel strikes South Pars gas field, world's largest natural gas reserve
description: The Israeli Air Force conducted airstrikes on Iran's South Pars natural gas field in the Persian Gulf, the world's single largest gas reservoir shared with Qatar. Multiple offshore platforms were hit, Iran declared a national energy emergency, and Trump privately complained to Netanyahu about the strike's impact on global energy prices.

title: Trump denounces NATO and Indo-Pacific allies for refusing military support
description: President Trump launched a public attack on NATO European members and Indo-Pacific allies including Japan and South Korea, accusing them of 'hiding behind America' while refusing to provide military support for operations against Iran. The statement further strained transatlantic relations already frayed by Trump's earlier unilateral action.

title: Israel launches ground invasion of southern Lebanon
description: Israeli ground forces crossed into southern Lebanon along a broad front, the most significant ground operation in Lebanon since 2006. Over one million people have been displaced by Israeli evacuation orders since March 2. IDF says the objective is to establish a security buffer zone and dismantle remaining Hezbollah rocket infrastructure.

title: Iran fires cluster munitions at Ramat Gan, killing 2 civilians
description: Iran fired rockets carrying cluster munitions that struck the Tel Aviv suburb of Ramat Gan, killing two civilians and wounding dozens. The use of cluster munitions drew international condemnation. Since the war began, Iran has fired over 500 ballistic missiles and nearly 2,000 drones at Israel and US targets.

title: Ali Larijani and Basij chief Gholamreza Soleimani killed in Israeli strikes
description: Former Iranian parliament speaker Ali Larijani and Basij Resistance Force commander General Gholamreza Soleimani were both killed in Israeli airstrikes on the same day, continuing the systematic dismantling of Iran's political and military leadership that began with Supreme Leader Khamenei's assassination on February 28.

title: IDF strikes Tehran nuclear program sites
description: Israeli Air Force jets conducted sustained strikes on multiple sites associated with Iran's nuclear program in the Tehran region, including enrichment facilities and research centers. The strikes built on earlier damage to Natanz on March 1, significantly degrading the physical infrastructure of Iran's nuclear program.

title: NATO European nations formally reject Trump's call for military support
description: NATO's European members formally declined President Trump's call to contribute military forces to operations against Iran, with a joint statement emphasizing diplomatic solutions. The rejection follows France's earlier condemnation of the strikes as 'outside international law' and underscores the deepening transatlantic rift.

title: France proposes Lebanon ceasefire plan including Arab-state recognition of Israel
description: French President Macron unveiled a comprehensive peace proposal involving an immediate ceasefire in Lebanon, a phased Israeli military withdrawal, and unprecedented Arab-state recognition of Israel as part of a broader regional normalization framework. The proposal was the most concrete diplomatic initiative to emerge since the war began.

title: IDF destroys aircraft assigned to Iran's Supreme Leader at Mehrabad Airport
description: Israeli Air Force struck and destroyed the aircraft designated for use by Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei at Tehran's Mehrabad Airport, eliminating a potential evacuation route for Iran's top leadership and sending a pointed symbolic message about Israel's intelligence reach.

title: IDF strikes Isfahan for second time, killing 15
description: Israeli Air Force conducted a major strike package targeting Isfahan, Iran's third-largest city, killing at least 15 people and destroying multiple weapons production facilities. This is the second major Israeli strike on Isfahan this month, the first having damaged UNESCO World Heritage sites in the city's historic center on March 9.

title: US claims Iran missile capability degraded 90%, drone arsenal 95%
description: The Pentagon released an assessment claiming that combined US-Israeli strikes had degraded Iran's ballistic missile production and stockpile capacity by 90% and its drone arsenal by 95%, including the destruction of 17 Iranian warships. Independent analysts questioned the figures, noting Iran continued launching hundreds of drones and missiles daily.

title: Royal Tulip Hotel Baghdad struck during EU-Saudi diplomatic delegation visit
description: A missile strike hit the Royal Tulip Hotel in Baghdad's Green Zone, where a high-level European Union and Saudi diplomatic delegation was meeting, killing three security personnel and wounding twelve others. The strike nearly brought European states directly into the conflict and prompted the EU to evacuate diplomatic staff from Baghdad.

title: Kata'ib Hezbollah commander Abu Ali al-Askari killed in Baghdad drone strike
description: Abu Ali al-Askari, the public-facing commander of Iran-backed Iraqi militia Kata'ib Hezbollah, was killed in a US drone strike in the Abu Ghraib area west of Baghdad. The Islamic Resistance in Iraq had claimed 67 drone and missile attacks on US bases in Iraq and Kurdistan since February 28.

title: Mojtaba Khamenei warns attacks will continue unless all US bases in region close
description: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, elected to lead Iran on March 8 after his father's assassination, issued a public declaration that Iranian military operations would continue and escalate unless all US military bases in the Middle East were shuttered. Khamenei has been governing while recovering from injuries sustained in the February 28 strike.

title: IDF launches Day 14 strikes: 200+ targets in Tehran, Shiraz, Ahvaz simultaneously
description: In one of the largest single-day strike packages of Operation Epic Fury, the IDF struck over 200 targets across Tehran, Shiraz, and Ahvaz simultaneously over a 6-hour period, targeting military command nodes, air defense sites, and missile production facilities. Iran's Health Ministry reports the death toll in Iran has now exceeded 1,444.

title: French soldier killed in Iranian UAV attack near Erbil, NATO considers response
description: A French soldier stationed at a NATO advisory mission near Erbil, Iraq, was killed when an Iranian Shahed-series drone struck the facility, marking the first NATO member state fatality in the conflict. The IDF on the same day confirmed that six US crew members had died when a refueling aircraft crashed in western Iraq under hostile fire.

title: CENTCOM confirms 6 US crew killed in refueling aircraft crash in western Iraq
description: US Central Command announced that all six crew members aboard a US refueling aircraft were killed when the aircraft crashed in western Iraq. The incident, which occurred during a combat support mission, raised the total number of US service members killed in action during Operation Epic Fury to more than a dozen.

title: IEA declares largest oil supply disruption in history; Brent at $101/barrel
description: The International Energy Agency stated that the 2026 Iran war has created the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market. Gulf countries have cut production by at least 10 million barrels per day. Brent crude stood at $101/barrel, up 39% from $72.48 before the war began on February 28. The IEA announced an emergency release of 400 million barrels from strategic reserves.

title: US strikes Kharg Island terminal to pressure Iran over Hormuz blockade
description: The US military launched overnight strikes targeting Kharg Island, Iran's critical oil export hub that handles roughly 90% of Iranian crude exports. Trump framed the attack as leverage to force Iranian officials to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran formally declared closed on March 4.

title: Iran lays mines in Strait of Hormuz; US sinks 16 Iranian minelayers
description: Iran began laying naval mines in the Strait of Hormuz using small craft capable of carrying two to three mines each. US intelligence estimated Iran possessed between 2,000 and 6,000 naval mines. The IRGC declared any vessel linked to Israel, the US, or their allies 'a legitimate target.' CENTCOM confirmed it destroyed 16 Iranian minelayers in the strait. Iranian forces also attacked at least six vessels with projectiles and explosive-laden unmanned surface craft, damaging tankers including a Marshall Islands-flagged ship and a Malta-flagged vessel carrying fuel from Iraq.

title: Hegseth declares 'most intense day' of strikes; Taleqan-2 nuclear facility at Parchin destroyed
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth declared March 11 'our most intense day of strikes inside Iran,' involving the most fighters, bombers, and strikes of the war. Satellite imagery confirmed the destruction of the Taleqan-2 facility in Iran's Parchin military complex, a key component of Iran's nuclear weapons program. Iran simultaneously launched 11 waves of ballistic missile attacks against Israel — the second-highest single-day total of the war. Ten missiles with multiple warheads targeted Tel Aviv, injuring 124 people; targets also included Palmachim Airbase, Ovda Airbase, and Shin Bet headquarters.

title: Lebanon's President Aoun calls for direct Lebanon-Israel negotiations and ceasefire
description: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun publicly proposed a political framework for ending the conflict: an immediate ceasefire, deployment of the Lebanese Army in southern friction zones, confiscation of Hezbollah's weapons depots, and direct Lebanon-Israel negotiations under international auspices. The proposal was the first formal Lebanese diplomatic initiative since the war resumed and was welcomed by France and the EU.

title: IDF strikes Isfahan historic center, damaging UNESCO World Heritage mosques
