description: Israeli airstrikes hit central Isfahan, damaging the UNESCO World Heritage-listed Naqsh-e Jahan Square, Chehel Sotoun palace, Ali Qapu gate, the Shah Mosque, and the Jameh Mosque. UNESCO issued an emergency statement condemning the strikes. Iran called the damage to its cultural heritage 'a war crime.'

title: Mojtaba Khamenei elected new Supreme Leader of Iran by Assembly of Experts
description: Iran's Assembly of Experts officially elected Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the assassinated Supreme Leader, as the new head of state under Iran's constitution. He was found to have survived the February 28 strike with injuries to his legs, hand, and arm. His election was followed by protests in Tehran with crowds chanting 'Death to Mojtaba.'

title: Iranian drone strikes Bahrain desalination plant, threatening Gulf water supply
description: Bahrain's Ministry of Interior confirmed an Iranian drone damaged a seawater desalination plant and injured three people, raising fears about attacks on civilian water infrastructure. Desalination provides virtually all drinking water in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Qatar. Iranian parliament speaker Qalibaf warned that desalination plants across the Gulf are 'legitimate targets' if Iranian power infrastructure is struck.

title: US-Israel destroy Shahed drone factory in Isfahan, cutting Iran-Russia supply chain
description: Satellite imagery published March 8 confirmed the complete destruction of the Shahed Aviation Industries Production Facility in Isfahan — Iran's primary manufacturer of Shahed-136, Shahed-129, and Shahed-171 attack drones. The facility had supplied drones to Russia for use against Ukraine. The strike was seen as simultaneously degrading both Iranian and Russian military capabilities.

title: Israel Navy strikes Ramada hotel in central Beirut, killing 5 IRGC Quds Force commanders
description: An Israeli Navy strike hit the Ramada Hotel in Beirut's Raouche neighbourhood — Israel's first attack on central Beirut since the war began — killing five senior IRGC Quds Force commanders. The IDF named three: Majid Hassani (responsible for transferring funds to Iranian proxies in Lebanon), and senior intelligence figures Alireza Bi-Azar and Ahmad Rasouli. Iran's UN mission confirmed four of the dead were 'diplomats of the Islamic Republic of Iran.' The hotel was also housing civilians displaced from southern Lebanon. The Lebanese government had days earlier ordered the arrest and repatriation of all IRGC personnel in Lebanon; over 150 Iranian nationals subsequently left the country.

title: Israel strikes Iranian oil infrastructure in Tehran for first time; 41 killed in Bekaa Valley
description: The Israeli Air Force struck Iranian oil infrastructure in Tehran on March 7 — the first strike on Iranian national civilian energy infrastructure since the war began on February 28. Simultaneously in Lebanon, at least 41 people were killed and 40 wounded in Israeli air-and-ground raids on Nabi Chit in the eastern Bekaa Valley, marking a significant northern expansion of Israeli operations. An Israeli commando unit infiltrated under cover of darkness before being engaged by Hezbollah fighters, triggering intense airstrikes. Israel said the operation was partly aimed at locating the remains of navigator Ron Arad, missing since 1986.

title: Trump administration bypasses Congress with emergency $650M arms sale: 20,000 bombs to Israel
description: The Trump administration used emergency executive authority to bypass the standard 30-day congressional review and expedite the sale of more than 20,000 bombs to Israel worth approximately $650 million. Secretary of State Marco Rubio determined an emergency existed and waived congressional notification requirements. The decision drew criticism from lawmakers who said it circumvented legislative oversight at a critical moment in the conflict.

title: UN declares major humanitarian emergency; 3.2 million Iranians displaced
description: The United Nations declared the Iran conflict a 'major humanitarian emergency,' estimating that up to 3.2 million people in Iran had been displaced from Tehran and other urban centers. The conflict zone hosted nearly 25 million people as refugees or displaced persons. The WHO identified 13 Iranian health infrastructure sites struck during the war, plus one health facility in Lebanon.

title: Macron condemns Israeli UNIFIL strike as 'unacceptable'; Western allies demand restraint
description: French President Macron called Israeli strikes on UNIFIL peacekeepers in Lebanon 'unacceptable,' as fresh attacks on UN positions were confirmed. France, Germany, Italy, the UK, and Canada issued a joint statement urging Israel to halt the ground invasion, warning of 'devastating humanitarian consequences.' India, which has troops in UNIFIL, also issued a formal protest. The pressure marked the sharpest Western rebuke of Israel since the war began.

title: Iran drones strike Azerbaijan's Nakhchivan exclave; airport hit, diplomats expelled
description: Two Iranian drones struck Azerbaijan's Nakhchivan exclave — one hitting the terminal of Nakhchivan International Airport, the other landing near a school in Shakarabad village — injuring two civilians. Azerbaijan immediately withdrew its diplomats from Iran and pledged a military response. Iran's Foreign Minister suggested the strikes were an Israeli false flag, but an IRGC-linked Telegram channel claimed responsibility.

title: Iran formally declares Strait of Hormuz closed; attacks on merchant shipping begin
description: Iranian forces formally declared the Strait of Hormuz closed to commercial shipping and began attacking vessels attempting to transit. Over 150 ships anchored outside the strait to avoid attack. The effective closure of the world's most critical oil chokepoint, through which 20 million barrels of oil per day normally flow, triggered an immediate global energy crisis.

title: Brent crude surges past $120/barrel as Iran formally closes Strait of Hormuz
description: Following Iran's formal declaration of Hormuz closure, Brent crude surged past $120/barrel — up 65% from $72.48 before the war. QatarEnergy had already declared force majeure on LNG exports on March 2 after initial strikes on Ras Laffan. The IEA stated the global oil market faces its worst disruption in history.

title: US submarine USS Charlotte sinks Iranian frigate IRIS Dena off Sri Lanka (87 killed)
description: The Los Angeles-class submarine USS Charlotte fired two Mark 48 torpedoes at the Iranian frigate IRIS Dena approximately 19 nautical miles off Galle, Sri Lanka, sinking it in under three minutes and killing 87 crew. It was the first time a US submarine had sunk a vessel in combat since World War II. The Dena was returning from India's MILAN 2026 naval exercises. Iran's Foreign Minister called it 'an atrocity at sea.'

title: Qatar arrests 10-person IRGC spy and sabotage cell operating in country
description: Qatar's security services arrested ten people suspected of operating as two IRGC-linked cells: seven assigned to intelligence collection on military infrastructure and three trained in drone use for sabotage operations. The arrests came as Qatar also shot down two Iranian Su-24 bombers earlier in the conflict — the first Iranian aircraft destroyed by any nation in the war.

title: Israel destroys Azadi Indoor Stadium in Tehran on Day 4 of Operation Epic Fury
description: An American-Israeli airstrike destroyed the Azadi Indoor Stadium in western Tehran, Iran's largest indoor sports arena. The strike was part of a broad campaign hitting cultural, sports, and civilian-adjacent infrastructure across Tehran. Iran's state media reported that over 500 ballistic missiles and nearly 2,000 drones had been fired at Israel and US targets since February 28.

title: France condemns US-Israel strikes as 'outside international law'
description: French President Macron stated France 'cannot approve' of the strikes being conducted 'outside of international law,' while simultaneously announcing the movement of French naval materiel into the Mediterranean. France's position — backing defensive measures but condemning offensive strikes — reflects the broader European attempt to distance itself from the conflict.

title: Israel launches ground incursion into southern Lebanon; UNIFIL peacekeepers injured
description: Israeli Defense Minister Katz authorized and launched a ground incursion into southern Lebanon using forces from the 91st Division, aiming to establish a security buffer and dismantle Hezbollah rocket infrastructure. The same day, two Israeli missile strikes hit a UNIFIL battalion headquarters in Bint Jbeil, injuring three Ghanaian peacekeepers and triggering a joint condemnation statement from France, the UK, Germany, Canada, and Italy.

title: Hezbollah enters war with rockets at Haifa; IDF calls it 'declaration of war'
description: Hezbollah launched missiles and drones into northern Israel targeting an IDF base in Haifa, calling it retaliation for Supreme Leader Khamenei's killing. The IDF spokesperson issued an emergency statement calling the attack 'an official declaration of war.' Lebanon's government banned further Hezbollah military activity, but the order was largely ignored.

title: US Embassy in Kuwait struck by Iranian missile; evacuated indefinitely
description: An Iranian missile struck the US Embassy compound in Kuwait, forcing its indefinite closure. No casualties were reported. On the same day, the US-flagged tanker Stena Imperative and the Honduras-flagged tanker Athe Nova were also struck in the Persian Gulf, marking the first attacks on commercial shipping in this conflict.

title: Israel strikes Arg Square in Tehran, damaging UNESCO-listed Golestan Palace
description: Israeli airstrikes on Arg Square in Tehran's historic center caused collateral damage to the nearby Golestan Palace, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. UNESCO issued an immediate statement of concern. The strike was part of Day 3 operations targeting government and security facilities in central Tehran.

title: Iranian drones strike Bahrain airport fuel tanks; three killed
description: Iranian drones struck fuel storage tanks at Bahrain International Airport, sending plumes of smoke over the capital Manama and killing three people. Bahrain condemned the strikes on the US Navy Fifth Fleet headquarters it hosts as 'a treacherous attack and blatant violation of sovereignty.' Kuwait and Qatar temporarily closed their airspace.

title: Global markets reel: Dow Jones drops 400+ points, oil surges to $80/barrel
description: Global stock markets fell sharply on the third day of the war as the scope of Iranian retaliation became clear. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell over 400 points. Brent crude jumped to $80/barrel, up from $72.48 before the war. Goldman Sachs raised its 2026 US inflation forecast by 0.8 percentage points and raised recession odds to 25%.

title: Kuwaiti F/A-18 accidentally shoots down 3 US F-15Es in major friendly fire incident
description: A Kuwaiti Air Force F/A-18 Hornet pilot fired three AIM-9 Sidewinder missiles and shot down three USAF F-15E Strike Eagles over Kuwait during a chaotic multi-threat intercept operation. All six US crew members survived after ejecting. The incident — the first loss of US aircraft in Operation Epic Fury — raised urgent questions about coalition IFF procedures and came hours after an Iranian drone killed six US soldiers at Shuaiba port.

title: UK, France, Germany back proportionate defensive measures against Iranian missiles
description: The E3 (UK, France, Germany) issued a joint statement resolving to back 'proportionate military defensive measures' against Iranian drones and ballistic missiles, while stopping short of endorsing offensive strikes on Iran. British Prime Minister Keir Starmer separately announced approval of US use of British bases for 'defensive' operations.

title: IAEA confirms Natanz nuclear facility struck; no radiological release detected
description: The International Atomic Energy Agency confirmed that satellite imagery showed entrance buildings at Iran's Natanz enrichment facility in Isfahan had been damaged by US-Israeli strikes. The IAEA stated that no radiological consequences were expected from the damage, but expressed concern about the precedent of strikes on nuclear infrastructure.

title: Iranian ballistic missiles strike Beit Shemesh in central Israel, killing 9
description: An Iranian ballistic missile barrage struck the central Israeli city of Beit Shemesh, killing nine people and injuring more than 20 in one of the deadliest single Iranian strikes on Israeli soil. Iran's IRGC claimed the attack was retaliation for the assassination of Supreme Leader Khamenei the previous day.

title: Iran establishes Interim Leadership Council after Khamenei's assassination
description: Under Iran's constitution, an Interim Leadership Council was established to exercise the functions of head of state following Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's assassination the previous day. Former security official Ali Larijani announced the council. Some Iranian cities saw celebrations as statues of Khamenei were toppled; the state declared 40 days of mourning.

title: Dubai International Airport struck by drone, briefly halts all flights
description: Dubai International Airport, the world's busiest by international passengers, was struck by a suspected drone in the early hours of March 1, injuring four staff and causing minor damage. The airport halted all flights and evacuated passengers to bomb shelters before resuming limited operations hours later. Airlines including Emirates suspended Middle East routes as airspace across the Gulf region was intermittently closed.

title: Iranian Armed Forces Chief of Staff General Abdolrahim Mousavi killed in strikes
description: General Abdolrahim Mousavi, Chief of Staff of the Iranian Armed Forces and the country's top uniformed military officer, was confirmed killed by Iranian state media on March 1. His death, alongside Khamenei's, left Iran's command structure in acute disarray during the critical early phase of the war. Iran declared 40 days of national mourning.

title: Iran attacks all six GCC states simultaneously for first time in history
description: In an unprecedented retaliation, Iran launched simultaneous strikes against all six Gulf Cooperation Council countries — Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE — targeting US bases and energy infrastructure. The IRGC specifically struck Al Udeid Air Base (Qatar), Ali Al Salem Air Base (Kuwait), Al Dhafra Air Base (UAE), and US Navy Fifth Fleet HQ (Bahrain).

title: Iranian strikes hit Dubai near Burj Khalifa; Fairmont The Palm hotel ablaze
description: Iranian missiles and drones struck residential and commercial areas of Dubai near the Burj Khalifa, Dubai Marina, and the Palm Jumeirah. The Fairmont The Palm hotel was set on fire, injuring four people. The UAE intercepted 169 of 182 detected ballistic missiles and 645 of 689 drones, but 44 projectiles hit within UAE territory.

title: IDF strikes Hezbollah tunnels and missile sites in southern Lebanon
description: Simultaneous with the opening strikes on Iran, the Israeli Air Force conducted preemptive strikes on Hezbollah tunnel shafts, rocket-launching sites, and detection equipment in southern Lebanon. The strikes were the last before Hezbollah entered the war on March 2 following Khamenei's assassination.

title: Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei assassinated in Israeli decapitation strike on Tehran
description: At 06:45 UTC, the Israeli Air Force began an unprecedented wave of decapitation strikes on Tehran. Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and IRGC commander Pakpour, Defense Minister Nasirzadeh, security adviser Ali Shamkhani, and Armed Forces Chief of Staff Bagheri were all killed. Khamenei's daughter, son-in-law, grandchild, and daughter-in-law were also killed. Iran declared 40 days of mourning.

title: US and Israel launch Operation Epic Fury: 900+ joint strikes on Iran in first 12 hours
description: At 06:35 UTC on February 28, CENTCOM announced the start of Operation Epic Fury (Israeli codename: 'Roaring Lion'). US warships fired Tomahawk missiles, B-2 stealth bombers and B-52 Stratofortresses struck fortified ballistic missile facilities, and US Army HIMARS launchers were deployed. Nearly 900 strikes hit Iran in the first 12 hours, targeting nuclear sites, air defenses, and command infrastructure.

title: Qatar shoots down 2 Iranian Su-24 bombers — first Iranian aircraft destroyed in war
description: Qatar's air defenses shot down two Iranian Su-24 Fencer bomber aircraft, becoming the first nation to destroy an Iranian manned aircraft in the conflict. Qatar's Patriot and Hawk batteries also intercepted 101 of 104 detected ballistic missiles and 24 of 39 drones fired at Qatari territory, though 15 struck within Qatar, including a residential building on the outskirts of Doha.

title: Oman FM confirms Iran agreed to nuclear zero-enrichment deal hours before war began
description: Oman's Foreign Minister disclosed that just hours before US-Israeli strikes began, Iran had agreed — through Omani mediation and indirect talks with VP Vance — to zero enriched uranium stockpiling and full IAEA verification. Trump's 'no aborts' order to CENTCOM came hours after Oman reported the breakthrough, effectively burying the deal. Oman later expressed 'deep disappointment' that active negotiations were abandoned.

title: US Embassy and CIA station in Riyadh struck by Iranian drones on Day 1
description: Iranian drones struck the US Embassy compound in Riyadh, causing fires and minor structural damage; the CIA station housed in the same complex was also hit. No casualties were reported. The State Department closed the Riyadh embassy indefinitely. Iran's IRGC stated the strike on the 'Washington embassy in Riyadh' was part of its campaign to destroy 'American political centers' across the region.

title: Trump: US 'totally obliterated' Kharg Island military sites in massive bombing raid
description: President Trump announced the US military had 'totally obliterated' military forces on Kharg Island in what he called 'one of the most powerful bombing raids in the History of the Middle East.' US CENTCOM confirmed a large-scale precision strike destroyed 90+ Iranian military sites on the island, including naval mine storage facilities, missile bunkers, and assets used to threaten Hormuz shipping. Trump said he deliberately spared oil infrastructure 'for reasons of decency.'

title: US weighing occupation or naval blockade of Kharg Island to reopen Hormuz
description: Axios reported that the Trump administration is considering plans to occupy or impose a naval blockade on Iran's Kharg Island — through which 90% of Iran's oil exports pass — to force Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Three sources said a ground occupation is under serious consideration, with 5,000 Marines and sailors deploying to the Persian Gulf. White House confirmed: 'The United States Military can take out Kharg Island at any time if the President gives the order.' Analysts warned a strike on oil infrastructure could push prices past $200/barrel.

title: US re-strikes Natanz enrichment facility with GBU-57 bunker-buster bombs
description: The United States struck Iran's Natanz uranium enrichment facility using GBU-57 bunker-buster bombs, designed to penetrate reinforced underground structures. Israel denied involvement, refusing to comment on US activities. Iran confirmed the strike. IAEA head Grossi confirmed 'some recent damage to entrance buildings' of the underground plant and called for 'military restraint to avoid any risk of a nuclear accident,' adding no radiation release was detected. Russia condemned the attack, with spokeswoman Zakharova warning it posed 'real risk of catastrophic disaster throughout the Middle East.'

title: UK confirms Iran fired two ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia; both failed to hit
description: The UK confirmed Iran fired two intermediate-range ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia, the joint US-UK military base in the Indian Ocean used to stage long-range B-52 bombers. One was intercepted by a US warship; the other failed in flight. Iran denied responsibility, calling it an 'Israeli false flag.' UK Ministry of Defence condemned Iran's 'reckless attacks.' The failed strike showed Iran may possess missiles exceeding its previously self-declared 2,000 km range limit — raising alarm that reversed, they could reach London.

title: US-Israel launch 'unprecedented' new wave of strikes on eastern Tehran
description: Israel and the United States launched a new wave of attacks described as 'unprecedented' by Al Jazeera's correspondent in Tehran, particularly targeting the eastern side of the city. Iran activated air defence systems in response to waves of US-Israeli drones. The Israeli military said it launched a second round of attacks within hours of announcing a 'wide-scale wave of strikes' on Tehran infrastructure. CENTCOM's Admiral Cooper said the campaign was 'ahead or on plan,' claiming 8,000+ targets struck and Iran's military capacity in 'steady decline.'

title: IRGC claims missile strike on Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia
description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps announced its forces struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia — a major US military installation hosting American personnel and aircraft used in the Iran campaign. Earlier in the war, US officials told the Wall Street Journal that five US Air Force refueling planes at the base were struck and damaged by Iranian missiles. Saudi Arabia has been intercepting multiple Iranian ballistic missile salvoes targeting the base throughout the conflict.

title: Russia's Lavrov urges Iran to end hostilities; warns Bushehr strikes 'extremely dangerous'
description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov spoke with Iran's Foreign Minister, urging immediate cessation of hostilities. Lavrov separately condemned US-Israeli strikes near Iran's Bushehr nuclear power plant as 'extremely dangerous,' warning of potential catastrophic consequences. CNN reported Russia has been providing Iran with real-time intelligence on US troop, ship, and aircraft movements — a development the Trump administration was 'taken by surprise' by. Russia also affirmed strong ties with Tehran through IRNA-reported statements.

title: IRGC claims missile and drone strike on US Fifth Fleet base in Bahrain
description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed it struck the US Navy's Fifth Fleet headquarters in Manama, Bahrain, using missiles and drones. Bahrain's Defense Force confirmed intercepting two drones and two ballistic missiles. The Fifth Fleet base, headquartered in the Juffair district, is the primary hub for US naval operations in the Gulf region and has been a repeated target since the war began on February 28.

title: Iran fires cluster-warhead missiles at Tel Aviv metro area; 6+ injured
description: Iran fired missiles equipped with cluster warheads at the Tel Aviv metropolitan area, with impacts recorded across central Tel Aviv, Ramat Gan, Petah Tikva, and Holon. At least six people were injured. Israeli police estimated one warhead contained approximately 220 pounds of explosives. Cluster munitions disperse into multiple submunitions over a wide area, significantly increasing civilian risk. The Israeli Knesset suspended a late-night session due to missile alerts. Since the war began, 18 Israelis have been killed and over 4,700 injured.

title: Iran launches 21 drones and 3 ballistic missiles at Saudi Arabia's Eastern Province oil facilities
description: Saudi Arabia's Ministry of Defense intercepted approximately 21 drones and three ballistic missiles targeting the Eastern Province — the heart of the kingdom's oil and gas infrastructure. One ballistic missile headed for Riyadh was intercepted; another fell in an uninhabited area. The IRGC claimed the strikes targeted energy installations supporting US military operations. Bahrain also sounded warning alarms multiple times as Iranian missiles and drones swept across the Gulf.

title: Lebanon declares Iran's ambassador persona non grata; orders departure by March 29
description: Lebanon's Foreign Ministry declared Iran's designated ambassador Mohammad Reza Sheibani persona non grata and ordered him to leave by March 29. Lebanon also recalled its own ambassador to Tehran for consultations. Foreign Minister Youssef Raggi cited Iran's violations of diplomatic norms and its role in dragging Lebanon into war through Hezbollah. Lebanon had already reimposed visa requirements on Iranian travellers and begun expelling individuals with IRGC ties. The decision marks a dramatic rupture in Lebanon-Iran relations.

title: UK deploys additional air defenses and warship to Middle East as Iranian missile threat escalates
description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced Britain is deploying short-range air defense systems and an additional naval vessel to the Middle East to counter Iranian missile and drone attacks. RAF Typhoons from No. 12 Squadron are operating from Al Udeid Air Base at Qatar's request, having already intercepted Iranian drones. The UK also deployed helicopters to help defend Cypriot airspace. The deployment marks a deepening of British military involvement beyond the Diego Garcia basing support already provided to US long-range bombers.

title: Mediators push Islamabad meeting between Ghalibaf and US envoys; April 9 set as war-end target
description: Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, Oman, and Qatar are working to arrange a face-to-face meeting in Islamabad between Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, with VP JD Vance potentially attending. An Israeli official confirmed Washington set April 9 as the target date for ending the war. Pakistan's army chief Asim Munir spoke directly with Trump. Iran's Supreme National Security Council had yet to approve the meeting. Israel said it was 'surprised things were moving that fast' when Trump revealed the contacts.

title: David's Sling malfunction allows two Iranian ballistic missiles to strike southern Israel
description: Israel's military confirmed a malfunction in its 'David's Sling' medium-to-long-range interceptor system allowed two Iranian ballistic missiles to breach Israel's multi-layered air defence shield and strike southern Israel, wounding dozens. David's Sling is designed to intercept ballistic missiles and long-range rockets and sits between the Iron Dome and Arrow systems in Israel's defence hierarchy. The failure exposed a critical gap in coverage.

title: US kills PMF commander Saad Dawai al-Baiji and 15 fighters in Anbar airstrike
description: A US airstrike in Iraq's Anbar province killed Saad Dawai al-Baiji, provincial commander and head of operations for the pro-Iranian Popular Mobilisation Forces (PMF / Hashed al-Shaabi), along with 15 fighters. The PMF, though formally part of Iraq's army, includes Iran-aligned factions. Al Jazeera's correspondent described Iraq as a secondary battleground where the US is conducting 'deliberate but calibrated' strikes against Iran-aligned group leaders, leaving Iraqis caught in the crossfire.

title: Iran acknowledges receiving US 'outreach' through intermediaries; proposals under review
description: An Iranian source told CNN there had been 'outreach' between the US and Tehran, and that Iran is willing to listen to 'sustainable' proposals to end the war — a softer line than its public denials. Iran's Foreign Ministry confirmed it received messages through friendly countries and said they were under review. Iran's position: it is ready to guarantee it will never develop nuclear weapons but demands all sanctions be lifted and requires binding guarantees against future US-Israeli strikes. Iran stated it is 'not asking for a meeting' but is 'willing to listen.'

title: Philippines declares national energy emergency; ~2,000 vessels trapped in Persian Gulf
description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. declared a 'national energy emergency' citing direct risks to domestic fuel supplies from the Hormuz closure. The International Maritime Organization reported approximately 2,000 vessels are now trapped inside the Persian Gulf, unable to transit the closed strait. Iran has selectively allowed ships from countries it considers neutral — including some Turkish and Indian vessels — to pass. GPS jamming of uncertain origin near the strait is additionally threatening safe navigation for oil tankers.

title: Houthis warn Bahrain and UAE: 'first to lose' if they help reopen Strait of Hormuz
description: Yemen's Houthi movement issued a direct threat to Bahrain and the UAE, warning they will be 'the first to lose in this battle' if they assist any effort to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The Houthis stated they would respond to any escalation against Iran, including maritime operations. Bahrain's forces have intercepted 153 missiles and 301 drones since the war began. The threat underscores how the conflict is creating a broader axis of pressure across the Arabian Peninsula.
